connector_candidate_ranking.recommendation: Next proof: targeted ext_proc/ext_authz runtime smoke with request blocking and explicit non-goals.
Run OpenLiteSpeed installation/licensing proof before adding a connector directory.
Prototype forwardAuth/decision-service feasibility before any Go plugin work.
Perform hook/proxy architecture spike before implementation.

connector_lifecycle.meaning: Candidate is tracked but no connector directory or selected runtime architecture is required yet.
connector_lifecycle.not_allowed_claims: No runtime support, no blocking support, no CRS support, no verified-case readiness.
connector_lifecycle.required_evidence: Roadmap entry, rationale, risks, and first proof definition.

connector_lifecycle.meaning: Connector directory or starter exists with README/metadata/build notes, but no real runtime proof.
connector_lifecycle.not_allowed_claims: No production readiness, no verified runtime result, no full-matrix eligibility.
connector_lifecycle.required_evidence: Repo-owned scaffold, origin notes, build or self-test starter, and explicit non-runtime disclaimer.

connector_lifecycle.meaning: Starter or adapter component compiles from clean checkout with documented command.
connector_lifecycle.not_allowed_claims: No traffic handling or ModSecurity semantics unless runtime logs prove them.
connector_lifecycle.required_evidence: Build command, artifact path outside checkout, source map, and passing syntax/lint checks.

connector_lifecycle.meaning: Server/proxy and any sidecar can start locally with minimal configuration.
connector_lifecycle.not_allowed_claims: No verified blocking or CRS coverage without a case result and logs.
connector_lifecycle.required_evidence: Start/stop or run script, minimal config, process logs, port allocation, and cleanup behavior.

connector_lifecycle.meaning: A targeted real runtime case can produce result.json and logs under verified runtime root.
connector_lifecycle.not_allowed_claims: No full-matrix readiness, no broad phase coverage, no production_verified status.
connector_lifecycle.required_evidence: result.json, case-run JSON/Markdown, access/error logs or equivalent, decision/audit evidence where applicable.

connector_lifecycle.meaning: Full-matrix jobs are technically schedulable for the connector, but may still fail or be incomplete.
connector_lifecycle.not_allowed_claims: No PASS/Merge Readiness claims until generated full-matrix evidence supports them.
connector_lifecycle.required_evidence: Matrix job definitions, runtime result producer, report integration, known limitation notes, and governance/lint pass.

connector_lifecycle.meaning: Connector passed the complete verified evidence pipeline and is included in production status.
connector_lifecycle.not_allowed_claims: Do not claim if any required generated evidence is blocked, stale, or missing.
connector_lifecycle.required_evidence: Verified runtime evidence, full matrix, governance, lint, quick-check, report layout, and merge-readiness PASS.

connector_lifecycle.meaning: Runtime is intentionally covered as a variant of an existing connector rather than a separate connector.
connector_lifecycle.not_allowed_claims: No separate full matrix, generated reports, or production connector identity.
connector_lifecycle.required_evidence: Decision record that names owning connector and allowed future compatibility-smoke path.

connector_lifecycle.meaning: Implementation cannot proceed until an external, licensing, architecture, or evidence blocker is resolved.
connector_lifecycle.not_allowed_claims: No forward status promotion until the blocker is removed and evidenced.
connector_lifecycle.required_evidence: Blocker description, owner/next proof, and what evidence would unblock it.

connector_status_matrix.why: Existing production connector with verified runtime and full-matrix coverage.

connector_status_matrix.next_step: Keep production coverage and optionally add OpenResty compatibility smoke under nginx later.
connector_status_matrix.why: Existing production connector with verified runtime and full-matrix coverage; OpenResty is NGINX-based.

connector_status_matrix.next_step: Keep production coverage and capability notes current.
connector_status_matrix.why: Existing production connector with verified runtime and full-matrix coverage; SPOE path is documented.

connector_status_matrix.next_step: Define and run a targeted ext_proc/ext_authz runtime smoke proof; do not start full matrix.
connector_status_matrix.why: Repository has an Envoy bridge starter and harness entrypoint, but no runtime-verified Envoy integration yet.

connector_status_matrix.next_step: Write a request-blocking feasibility note and select native-module versus sidecar/proxy architecture.
connector_status_matrix.why: Repository has a lighttpd bridge starter, but no native module/FastCGI/SCGI runtime integration.

connector_status_matrix.next_step: Prototype forwardAuth/decision-service feasibility before any Go plugin implementation.
connector_status_matrix.why: Repository has a decision-service starter, but no Traefik Go plugin/middleware or runtime harness.

connector_status_matrix.next_step: Run OpenLiteSpeed install/start proof with one CRS/request-blocking smoke if automation allows it.
connector_status_matrix.why: No repository directory exists; LiteSpeed/OpenLiteSpeed is only a candidate until install/licensing proof exists.

connector_status_matrix.next_step: Treat as nginx runtime variant or compatibility smoke only; no separate reports or full matrix.
connector_status_matrix.why: OpenResty is based on the NGINX runtime stack and should not fork connector ownership now.

envoy_architecture_options.ci_testability: good after pinned Envoy binary/container and deterministic ports
envoy_architecture_options.cons: Requires protobuf/gRPC service and careful body processing-mode limits; response-body intervention semantics need proof.
envoy_architecture_options.description: Envoy External Processing gRPC service that can inspect configured request/response processing points.
envoy_architecture_options.evidence: Envoy logs, processor logs, decision log, result.json, case-run files
envoy_architecture_options.intervention_status: map ModSecurity disruptive intervention to Envoy processor response/denied status
envoy_architecture_options.pros: Best fit for staged request/body/response experiments; external process can own ModSecurity lifecycle and evidence logs.
envoy_architecture_options.recommendation: primary proof path together with a minimal request-blocking case
envoy_architecture_options.request_blocking: yes, if the processor returns a denied/modified response before upstream forwarding
envoy_architecture_options.request_body: possible, subject to Envoy processing mode and buffering settings
envoy_architecture_options.response_body: possible in concept, but not claimed until explicitly evidenced

envoy_architecture_options.cons: Primarily authorization-oriented; request body/response body coverage is limited and not a full ModSecurity phase mapping.
envoy_architecture_options.description: Envoy authorization service that returns allow/deny before routing to upstream.
envoy_architecture_options.evidence: Envoy logs, authz service logs, decision log, result.json, case-run files
envoy_architecture_options.intervention_status: map deny decision to ext_authz denied response status 403
envoy_architecture_options.pros: Simple request blocking proof; easy to reason about 403 decisions and logs.
envoy_architecture_options.recommendation: acceptable first smoke if ext_proc is too heavy; pair with clear body/response non-goals
envoy_architecture_options.request_body: limited/config-dependent; do not claim broad request-body support in first proof
envoy_architecture_options.response_body: no practical response-body claim for first proof

envoy_architecture_options.ci_testability: moderate to poor for first proof
envoy_architecture_options.cons: Higher toolchain complexity; embedding libmodsecurity or a robust bridge is risky; evidence and debugging are harder.
envoy_architecture_options.description: Proxy-Wasm HTTP filter embedded in Envoy filter chain.
envoy_architecture_options.evidence: Envoy logs plus WASM module logs; more complex CI artifacts
envoy_architecture_options.intervention_status: filter must translate decisions into local responses/stream actions
envoy_architecture_options.pros: Native filter-chain placement and possible phase visibility.
envoy_architecture_options.recommendation: defer until sidecar proof confirms required semantics

envoy_architecture_options.ci_testability: good for a smoke, weak for connector semantics
envoy_architecture_options.cons: Not a strong long-term ModSecurity integration; body and response handling are constrained.
envoy_architecture_options.description: Envoy Lua HTTP filter that calls or models an external decision service.
envoy_architecture_options.evidence: Envoy logs, Lua log messages, sidecar logs if used
envoy_architecture_options.intervention_status: Lua script returns local 403 or delegates to sidecar
envoy_architecture_options.recommendation: use only as fallback feasibility spike, not preferred connector path

envoy_architecture_options.cons: Does not prove an Envoy connector; ModSecurity decision belongs to downstream connector.
envoy_architecture_options.description: Envoy fronts an existing verified connector such as nginx or apache.
envoy_architecture_options.evidence: Envoy forwarding logs plus existing connector logs
envoy_architecture_options.intervention_status: downstream connector returns status; Envoy only forwards it
envoy_architecture_options.pros: Fast compatibility smoke and infrastructure proof.
envoy_architecture_options.recommendation: allowed only as infrastructure smoke, not as Envoy connector evidence
envoy_architecture_options.request_blocking: yes, but by the existing connector rather than Envoy
envoy_architecture_options.risk: low for smoke, high if misrepresented

envoy_architecture_options.ci_testability: good if implemented as deterministic local process
envoy_architecture_options.cons: Protocol mapping and body buffering still must be proven; not a connector by itself.
envoy_architecture_options.description: Standalone service owns ModSecurity transaction evaluation; Envoy calls it through ext_proc/ext_authz or another control point.
envoy_architecture_options.evidence: service decision log, ModSecurity audit/decision log, Envoy logs, result.json
envoy_architecture_options.intervention_status: service returns intervention_status and decision fields consumed by Envoy adapter layer
envoy_architecture_options.pros: Clear separation of Envoy harness and ModSecurity lifecycle; reusable for Traefik/lighttpd sidecar studies.
envoy_architecture_options.recommendation: use as shared service behind ext_proc/ext_authz proof

envoy_proof_exit_criteria.failure_mode: If startup is flaky, the proof remains skeleton/runtime-startable work only.
envoy_proof_exit_criteria.required_evidence: Envoy, upstream, and decision service start from a deterministic script and clean up ports/processes.

envoy_proof_exit_criteria.failure_mode: If upstream pass-through fails, no ModSecurity-style decision claim is allowed.
envoy_proof_exit_criteria.required_evidence: A benign request reaches the upstream through Envoy and logs the route.

envoy_proof_exit_criteria.failure_mode: If only the upstream blocks, the result is an infrastructure smoke, not Envoy connector proof.
envoy_proof_exit_criteria.required_evidence: A known malicious smoke request returns HTTP 403 via the selected ext_proc/ext_authz path.

envoy_proof_exit_criteria.failure_mode: If decision evidence is missing, the HTTP status is insufficient for verified-case readiness.
envoy_proof_exit_criteria.required_evidence: Decision-service log records deny, intervention_status=403, case id, and request correlation id.

envoy_proof_exit_criteria.failure_mode: If artifacts live only in the checkout or are incomplete, do not promote the proof.
envoy_proof_exit_criteria.required_evidence: result.json, case-run.md, case-run.json, Envoy logs, and decision logs under $VERIFIED_RUN_ROOT/envoy-smoke/.

envoy_proof_exit_criteria.failure_mode: Any Full-Matrix or production claim invalidates the first-proof boundary.

future_connector_deliverables.evidence_rule: Required before any connector is listed as more than planned.
future_connector_deliverables.purpose: Describe connector scope, current lifecycle stage, build/runtime commands, and explicit non-claims.
Track source ownership, upstream references, and which files are repo-owned.

future_connector_deliverables.evidence_rule: Config presence is not runtime evidence until a run writes logs and result.json.
future_connector_deliverables.purpose: Provide minimal deterministic proxy/server configuration for local smoke runs.

future_connector_deliverables.evidence_rule: Must write artifacts under verified runtime roots, not inside the checkout.
future_connector_deliverables.purpose: Start, exercise, and clean up the minimal proof harness.

future_connector_deliverables.evidence_rule: A smoke target alone is not Full-Matrix integration.
future_connector_deliverables.purpose: Expose a small local smoke for developers and CI feasibility.

future_connector_deliverables.evidence_rule: Requires result.json, logs, case-run files, and truthful expected/actual status.
future_connector_deliverables.purpose: Run one real case through the verified-case evidence shape.

future_connector_deliverables.evidence_rule: Must be generated, never hand-edited, and must not be present for roadmap-only candidates.
future_connector_deliverables.purpose: Summarize real runtime evidence once a generator consumes verified inputs.

future_connector_deliverables.evidence_rule: Schedulable is not PASS; report actual status only after real execution.
future_connector_deliverables.purpose: Run a schedulable connector/CRS/MRTS matrix job.

future_connector_deliverables.evidence_rule: Do not fabricate job rows for connectors that are not integrated.
future_connector_deliverables.purpose: Record which jobs exist, completed, failed, or are missing.

future_connector_deliverables.evidence_rule: Roadmap-only reports must not influence readiness.
future_connector_deliverables.purpose: Expose final readiness only after critical generated evidence is complete.

lighttpd_traefik_feasibility.blocker: No selected native ModSecurity integration, FastCGI/SCGI bridge, or runtime harness.
lighttpd_traefik_feasibility.first_proof_step: Request-blocking feasibility proof that selects native module versus proxy/sidecar architecture.

lighttpd_traefik_feasibility.blocker: No Go plugin/middleware, no forwardAuth runtime harness, and no libmodsecurity lifecycle proof.
lighttpd_traefik_feasibility.first_proof_step: forwardAuth/decision-service returns 403 for a known malicious request with logs and result.json.

litespeed_candidate.edition_note: OpenLiteSpeed is likely more CI-friendly; LiteSpeed Enterprise may add license/download automation risk.
litespeed_candidate.evidence_required: install log, start log, minimal config, request/response transcript, result.json, access/error/audit logs if available.
litespeed_candidate.first_proof: OpenLiteSpeed install/start proof plus one CRS/request-blocking smoke, if automation and licensing allow it.
litespeed_candidate.install_path: Prefer package/container proof if license and automation permit it.
litespeed_candidate.main_risk: License/download automation, edition differences, ModSecurity-engine compatibility, package availability, and CI reproducibility.
litespeed_candidate.modsecurity_crs_support: Must be proven with the selected edition; do not assume libmodsecurity-v3 connector parity.
litespeed_candidate.not_allowed_claims: No production status, no full matrix, no CRS compatibility claim, and no phase coverage claim before evidence exists.

metadata.generated_notice: Generated file - do not edit manually.

new_connector_acceptance_criteria: request body smoke or documented not-supported reason

new_connector_phases.entry_condition: Candidate appears in repository skeletons, backlog, or architecture discussion.
new_connector_phases.exit_evidence: Roadmap-only generated report and optional architecture/onboarding docs.
new_connector_phases.name: Roadmap triage
new_connector_phases.promotion_gate: No runtime promotion; this phase only authorizes a proof plan.
new_connector_phases.work: Record status, rationale, risks, first proof, and forbidden claims.

new_connector_phases.entry_condition: Candidate has enough technical surface to compare integration options.
new_connector_phases.exit_evidence: Documented proof spec with acceptance criteria and rollback/cleanup expectations.
new_connector_phases.name: Architecture proof specification
new_connector_phases.promotion_gate: A proof may be implemented, but no Full-Matrix job may be added yet.
new_connector_phases.work: Choose one minimal control point, name alternatives, define logs, result schema, and non-goals.

new_connector_phases.entry_condition: Proof spec is accepted and can run without production connector changes.
new_connector_phases.exit_evidence: result.json, case-run files, access/error logs or equivalent, and decision/audit logs where applicable.
new_connector_phases.name: Targeted runtime smoke
new_connector_phases.promotion_gate: The smoke proves only the named case and must keep full_matrix_ready=false.
new_connector_phases.work: Create minimal config, launcher, upstream, decision service, and one blocking smoke.

new_connector_phases.entry_condition: Targeted smoke is deterministic and uses verified runtime paths.
new_connector_phases.exit_evidence: A real verified-case run for one case, with rerun command and artifact locations.
new_connector_phases.name: Verified-case readiness
new_connector_phases.promotion_gate: May become a full-matrix candidate only after governance, lint, quick-check, and evidence layout pass.
new_connector_phases.work: Wire the connector into the verified-case runner shape with documented capabilities and limitations.

new_connector_phases.entry_condition: Verified-case evidence exists and missing capability claims are explicitly documented.
new_connector_phases.exit_evidence: Full-Matrix jobs are schedulable and report completeness can describe generated evidence honestly.
new_connector_phases.name: Full-Matrix candidacy
new_connector_phases.promotion_gate: No PASS, readiness, or production claim until complete generated Full-Matrix evidence exists.
new_connector_phases.work: Add matrix job plumbing and generated-report integration without altering expected statuses.

new_connector_phases.entry_condition: Full-Matrix evidence is complete and critical reports are generated from verified inputs.
new_connector_phases.exit_evidence: Merge Readiness PASS, critical mismatches 0, governance/lint/quick-check/layout PASS.
new_connector_phases.name: Production verification
new_connector_phases.promotion_gate: Only now may the connector be described as production_verified.
new_connector_phases.work: Run the complete verified evidence pipeline and resolve mismatches through real fixes only.

new_connector_work_items.acceptance_criteria: Roadmap and onboarding docs name protocol, evidence, and blocked claims.
Can start Envoy and upstream locally without full-matrix integration.
Schema includes connector, case, expected/actual status, decision, intervention_status, evidence_scope, and full_matrix_ready=false.
Documents license/download path, automation risk, and one request-blocking proof fixture.
No separate connector, no separate full matrix, no separate generated reports.
Identifies first request-blocking hook and evidence that would prove it.
Known malicious request returns 403 with decision logs in targeted proof.

not_allowed_before_full_matrix: runtime capability claims without result.json and logs

goals: Envoy starts locally with a deterministic minimal config.
A simple upstream responds through Envoy.
A ModSecurity-like decision service or sidecar can emit a deny decision.
A case such as action_deny_phase1 or envoy_request_blocking_smoke returns HTTP 403.
Logs and decision evidence are written under the verified runtime root.
No full-matrix integration is added for this proof.
recommended_envoy_proof.name: Minimal Envoy ext_proc/ext_authz runtime smoke
non_goals: No CRS support in first proof.
No MRTS support in first proof.
recommended_envoy_proof.scope: targeted proof only; not a full connector and not a full-matrix producer

roadmap_scope.does_not_replace: runtime evidence, verified-case result.json evidence, or full-matrix evidence
roadmap_scope.evaluates: repository structure, existing skeletons, technical feasibility, first proof steps, and evidence gates

why_envoy_next.detail: The repo already contains an Envoy connector directory, bridge starter, metadata, and smoke entrypoint, so the next step can be a focused proof instead of an unbounded scaffold.
why_envoy_next.evidence_boundary: Existing files prove only a skeleton; they do not prove runtime behavior.

why_envoy_next.detail: Envoy has documented HTTP extension points such as ext_proc and ext_authz that can return an intervention-style deny before upstream routing.
why_envoy_next.evidence_boundary: The roadmap may recommend these surfaces, but support starts only after a targeted case writes result.json and logs.

why_envoy_next.detail: A small external decision service can also inform later Traefik forwardAuth and lighttpd sidecar feasibility work.
why_envoy_next.evidence_boundary: Reusable architecture is a planning advantage, not a Full-Matrix or CRS support claim.

why_envoy_next.detail: A single request-blocking smoke can answer whether Envoy can carry a ModSecurity-style deny decision without touching production connectors.
why_envoy_next.evidence_boundary: The first proof is targeted and must keep full_matrix_ready=false.
why_envoy_next.reason: High value with bounded first proof
